codeReview.summary: The cart page implementation fully covers all requirements from the feature description and expectedBehavior. The page at /cart includes: (1) 'Your Cart' heading, (2) empty state with centered message and CONTINUE SHOPPING button linking to /shop, (3) two-column desktop layout with line items (product image 80x80px, name, subtitle, quantity controls with border, unit price, line total, X remove button) and sticky order summary (subtotal, $11.95 delivery, total, CHECKOUT button logging cart data, Continue Shopping link). Mobile layout stacks to single column. Cart reads from CartContext, quantity changes call updateQuantity(), remove calls removeItem(). The worker also fixed a legitimate CartContext localStorage hydration bug by replacing useSyncExternalStore with a simpler lazy state initializer pattern. The fix is sound — it reads localStorage once on mount and persists via useEffect. The noted dev-mode hydration warning is expected and cosmetic only. Code follows all project conventions: zero border-radius, proper Tailwind tokens, Next.js Image component, TypeScript strict mode, proper accessibility labels on interactive elements.

issues.description: The empty state Button uses `as="a" href="/shop"` which renders a plain <a> tag instead of Next.js <Link>. This means no client-side navigation (full page reload). However, this is consistent with the project's established pattern per AGENTS.md ('Prefer <Button as="a" href="/path"> over nesting <Link><Button>'). The Button component itself doesn't use Next.js Link internally. This is a design-level decision, not a bug.

Desktop column header uses a 5-column CSS grid (md:grid-cols-[1fr_120px_120px_120px_40px]) that appears only at md breakpoint, while the actual two-column cart layout switches at lg breakpoint. This means at md width (768-1023px), the column headers show in a single-column layout context. The visual impact is minimal since the items still render correctly, but the header may look slightly disconnected from items on tablet-width screens.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The Button component renders a plain <a> tag for as="a" rather than Next.js <Link>, which means all CTA-style link buttons cause full page reloads. AGENTS.md documents the pattern ('Prefer <Button as="a" href="/path">') but doesn't note the navigation trade-off. If client-side navigation is desired, the Button component would need to integrate Next.js Link internally.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/components/ui/Button.tsx:56-60 — renders plain <a> tag. Used in CartContent.tsx:46 for CONTINUE SHOPPING link.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker's skillFeedback suggests documenting that agent-browser click with text selectors can time out, and @ref clicking via snapshot is more reliable. This is actionable skill feedback for the frontend-worker skill.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback.suggestedChanges: 'The agent-browser click command times out on some button text selectors; using snapshot + @ref clicking is more reliable.'

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker fixed a pre-existing CartContext bug (localStorage hydration clearing cart on navigation) as part of implementing the cart page feature. The fix changed from useSyncExternalStore to a lazy useState initializer. This is a valid approach but introduces an expected dev-mode hydration mismatch warning. The cart architecture docs in library/architecture.md should note this known dev-mode behavior.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/lib/cart-context.tsx diff shows removal of useSyncExternalStore pattern. Handoff discoveredIssues documents the hydration mismatch.

summary: Cart page implementation passes review. All feature requirements are met: empty state, line items with images/names/subtitles/quantity controls/remove buttons, sticky order summary with subtotal/delivery/total, checkout button logging to console, responsive layout. The CartContext localStorage hydration fix is a sound improvement. Two non-blocking observations: (1) Button as="a" uses plain <a> instead of Next.js Link (consistent with project pattern but loses client-side nav), (2) desktop column headers show at md breakpoint while cart layout switches at lg. Neither issue is blocking.
